steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Use the two facts as points instead of doing more algebra than needed. At birth, $x=0$ and the goat weighs 5 pounds, so one point is $(0,5)$. One day later, it has gained $\frac{1}{4}$ pound, so another point is $(1,5.25)$. In Desmos, put those into a table and run a linear regression; it returns a slope of $0.25$ and an intercept of 5.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content: Now compare the Desmos regression line, $y=0.25x+5$, to the choices. Choice A has the correct slope but y-intercept $-5$, so it misses the birth weight. Choice B has slope $\frac{1}{4}$ and y-intercept 5, matching both the daily gain and the birth weight. Choice C has the wrong slope and wrong intercept. Choice D has the right intercept but a negative slope, which would mean the goat loses weight. That matches choice B, so B is correct.
